profile.name: Abelliuxl
profile.bio: Licensed Doctor, Unlicensed AI Therapist. I build small, practical tools around medicine, games, automation, and AI.
profile.background: This is my personal Macintosh-style workspace: part portfolio, part toolbox, part lab notebook. Most of the things here are built to solve a real annoyance first, then kept small enough to understand and maintain.

projects.title: Chatbutte
projects.description: A standalone web app hosted on this server. Open it from here when you want the chat workspace.

projects.title: WOW Pal Data
projects.description: A World of Warcraft data page hosted as a separate mini-site on server94.

projects.title: weauto
projects.description: A macOS OCR-based WeChat robot service for local automation experiments.

projects.title: HideDesktopIcon
projects.description: A lightweight macOS menu bar tool for hiding and showing desktop icons.

projects.title: CopyEnglishName
projects.description: A World of Warcraft addon for copying English names of items, spells, quests, and other game data.

projects.title: TUNTray
projects.description: A Windows tray GUI for starting, stopping, and switching tun2socks proxy servers.

writings.title: Tools should stay small
writings.excerpt: The best personal tools usually start with one concrete problem and keep their shape close to the workflow.

writings.title: Medicine, games, and automation
writings.excerpt: My projects tend to sit between clinical thinking, World of Warcraft data, local automation, and practical AI helpers.

writings.title: A quiet desktop for noisy ideas
writings.excerpt: This page keeps the old Macintosh metaphor because a simple desktop still works well for a personal control panel.

notes.title: Current Focus
notes.content: Building practical AI and automation tools that are useful before they are impressive.

notes.title: Public Work
items: World of Warcraft addons and data utilities
Local automation for macOS and Windows
Small web apps that can live on a personal server

notes.title: Rules
items: Make the tool solve one real problem
Keep deployment simple enough to remember
Prefer readable systems over clever ones
